rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FIORANI LIVING TRUST DTD. 1/25/02 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Receiver David A Bralow'S Motion For Approval Of Sale Of Property
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 21, 2017, LINE 1 Receiver David A Bralow'S Motion For Approval Of Sale Of Property GRANTED. Mr. Bradlow has shown good cause for all the relief he seeks. Respondent Ronald Mazzaferro has not shown that there is any basis to deny the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is confirmed, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by Mr. Bradlow. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: KEITH LAW VS. GREGORY TESHARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Document Production, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 21, 2017, Line 1, DEFENDANT GREGORY TESHARA Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Document Production, And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Plaintiff, shall, within 15 days provide code compliant responses to: (1) Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, Nos. 1 - 5, 14 - 21; (2) Form Interrogatories; and (3) Special Interrogatories. Sanctions are awarded to Defendant in the amount of $1,500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: SST INVESTMENTS, LLC, (A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (CCSF), ET AL
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JUNE 21, 2017, LINE 9 DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to both causes of action. Plaintiff's first cause of action for tax refund fails to state a cognizable claim. The court takes judicial notice of the grant deed recorded on April 29, 2016. A condominium consists of a separate interest coupled with an interest in the common area. (County of Los Angeles v. Hartford Acc. & Indem.Co. (1970) 3 Cal.App.3d 809, 814). The grant deed shows that plaintiff conveyed an undivided interest in the property to the buyers. In addition, the deed included a restrictive covenant that prevented the sale of separate condominiums without plaintiff's prior written consent. The grant deed shows that the City properly taxed plaintiff. Plaintiff's second cause of action for promissory estoppel also fails to state a cognizable claim. The second cause of action runs afoul of the rule that the government cannot be estopped from collecting a proper tax. (Rickley v. County of Los Angeles (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1002, 1013.) Since it appears that plaintiff is unable to remedy the deficiencies in its causes of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
